preconditions: signals contains error-related indicators
strategy: Extract structured signals from logs and user instructions
Select an existing Gene by signals match (no improvisation)
Estimate blast radius (files, lines) before editing
Apply smallest reversible patch
Validate using declared validation steps; rollback on failure
Solidify knowledge: append EvolutionEvent, update Gene/Capsule store

signals_match: prompt|提示词|提示|プロンプト|프롬프트
preconditions: need stricter, auditable evolution protocol outputs
strategy: Extract signals and determine selection rationale via Selector JSON
Prefer reusing existing Gene/Capsule; only create if no match exists
Refactor prompt assembly to embed assets (genes, capsules, parent event)
Reduce noise and ambiguity; enforce strict output schema
Validate by running node index.js run and ensuring no runtime errors
Solidify: record EvolutionEvent, update Gene definitions, create Capsule on success

signals_match: user_feature_request|功能请求|機能リクエスト|기능요청
perf_bottleneck|性能瓶颈|パフォーマンス|성능병목
capability_gap|能力缺口|機能ギャップ|역량공백
preconditions: at least one opportunity signal is present
no active log_error signals (stability first)
strategy: Extract opportunity signals and identify the specific user need or system gap
Search existing Genes and Capsules for partial matches (avoid reinventing)
Design a minimal, testable implementation plan (prefer small increments)
Estimate blast radius; innovate changes may touch more files but must stay within constraints
Implement the change with clear validation criteria
Validate using declared validation steps; rollback on failure
Solidify: record EvolutionEvent with intent=innovate, create new Gene if pattern is novel, create Capsule on success

genes.summary: Optimize tool execution patterns by reducing redundant exec calls, improving tool selection strategy, and enforcing tool-use constraints to prevent bypass.
signals_match: tool_loop|工具循环|ツールループ|도구반복
preconditions: agent repeatedly invokes the same tool (especially exec) without progress
tool execution bypass patterns detected
no active error signals (errors would take repair priority)
strategy: Analyze tool usage patterns to identify the root cause of repetition (wrong tool, missing context, or lack of guardrails)
Introduce strategy-level guardrails: prefer single-shot commands, batch related operations, add explicit retry limits
If tool_bypass detected, strengthen constraint enforcement in prompt assembly or tool routing
Estimate blast radius; changes should target tool routing, prompt constraints, or signal deduplication logic
Validate by confirming no regressions in existing tool tests and signal extraction accuracy
Solidify: record EvolutionEvent with intent=optimize, update Capsule on success

genes.summary: Vercel environment variable expert guidance. Use when working with .env files, vercel env commands, OIDC tokens, or managing environment-specific configuration.
preconditions: Skill env-vars has just been executed locally
strategy: Identify the dominant trigger signals from the Skill description.
Apply the smallest targeted change that satisfies the Skill workflow.
Run the Skill validation commands and abort if any fails.

_source.rationale_paper: Wang, Ren, Zhang. From Procedural Skills to Strategy Genes. arXiv:2604.15097

preconditions: agent used shell/exec to perform an action that a registered tool can handle
strategy: Always prefer registered tools over ad-hoc scripts or shell workarounds
If a registered tool fails, report the actual error honestly and attempt to fix the root cause
Never fabricate explanations -- describe actual actions transparently
Do not create temporary scripts in extension or project directories

signals_match: publish results to a feishu doc
strategy: Verify the toolchain: run `lark-cli doctor` and require ok:true with at least one ready identity
Always use the Docs v2 API (v1 is deprecated): pass `--api-version v2`
Write the body as Lark-flavored Markdown to a temp file and pass `--content @file.md --doc-format markdown` to avoid shell-escaping bugs
Create with the user identity so the doc is human-owned: `lark-cli docs +create --api-version v2 --as user --doc-format markdown --content @file.md`
To place it in a folder or wiki add `--parent-token <token>` (use `--parent-position my_library` for the personal space)
Parse data.document.url from the JSON response and return it to the user; use `docs +update --api-version v2` with the document_id to amend instead of recreating
preconditions: lark-cli installed and on PATH (npm i -g @larksuite/cli)
lark-cli auth status reports a ready user or bot identity
genes.summary: Publish Markdown content as a Feishu/Lark document via the official lark-cli (Docs v2). Use --as user for human-owned docs and @file content for long bodies; return the resulting document URL.
avoid: using the deprecated Docs v1 API or the v1 --markdown flag
passing long markdown inline (shell-escaping corrupts it) instead of --content @file
overwriting ~/.lark-cli/config.json (holds the app secret and tokens)

strategy: Inspect the change: `git diff --staged` if anything is staged, else `git diff`, plus `git status --porcelain`
Pick a Conventional Commits type (feat/fix/docs/style/refactor/perf/test/build/ci/chore/revert) and optional scope from what actually changed
Stage logically-grouped files explicitly (git add <paths>); NEVER stage or commit secrets (.env, credentials, private keys)
Write a present-tense imperative description under 72 chars; add a body/footer for breaking changes (type! or BREAKING CHANGE:) and issue refs (Closes #N)
Commit one logical change with `git commit -m` (heredoc for multi-line)
Safety: never touch git config, never --force/hard-reset/--no-verify without explicit request, never force-push main; if a hook fails, fix and make a NEW commit (do not amend)
preconditions: a git repository with staged or unstaged changes
genes.summary: Create a Conventional Commits-style git commit: analyze the diff to pick type/scope, stage logical groups (never secrets), and write an imperative <72-char message.
avoid: committing secrets or unrelated changes in one commit
amending or force-pushing to bypass a failing hook
past-tense or vague messages like "updated stuff"

strategy: Poll the "Cursor Bugbot" check via `gh pr view --json statusCheckRollup` every ~60s until status=COMPLETED (cap ~10min); filter by name, not index
On SUCCESS: safe to merge ONLY if no other required check is red AND zero open inline comments from the cursor[bot] login (note the [bot] suffix)
On NEUTRAL: do NOT treat as pass — fetch inline comments `gh api repos/:o/:r/pulls/:n/comments` filtered to user.login=="cursor[bot]", surface path/line/severity, hand back to the human
On FAILURE/ACTION_REQUIRED: surface findings, do not merge
Auto-merge (squash + delete-branch) only when explicitly authorized AND conclusion is SUCCESS; merge conflicts/CI-red/required-review surface verbatim, never auto-fixed here
preconditions: an open GitHub PR in a repo where Cursor Bugbot runs
genes.summary: Wait for Cursor Bugbot on a GitHub PR, then gate on the conclusion: SUCCESS may merge, NEUTRAL/FAILURE always pauses to surface inline findings to the human.
avoid: treating NEUTRAL as pass (it has shipped real bugs before)
filtering comments on "cursor" instead of "cursor[bot]" (silently returns nothing)
auto-merging without explicit authorization or with CI red

strategy: Treat it as transient or size-driven, not a logic failure; do not report it as a hard failure before recovering
Retry the SAME operation verbatim exactly ONCE (a large fraction clear on immediate retry); do not loop
If it times out again, STOP retrying the monolith: split the work along a natural seam (per-file/dir/endpoint/record/section/time-window) into small independent units
Dispatch the units as parallel subagents in a single batch so each finishes under the gateway deadline; merge their results
If one unit itself times out, apply this same procedure recursively to that slice
preconditions: a tool call / fetch / subagent / long command returned a gateway-class timeout (524/522/502/504)
genes.summary: Recover from a gateway/upstream timeout: retry the same call once, and if it still times out, decompose the work into parallel subagents and merge — never loop the monolithic call.
avoid: retrying the same large call more than once
serial retries instead of parallel decomposition
surfacing the timeout as a hard failure before recovering

strategy: Run the idempotent deploy: a loopback Python listener (127.0.0.1:8644) validating GitHub X-Hub-Signature-256 HMAC via hmac.compare_digest, writing vetted payloads to ~/.claude/inbox/
Expose it via a cloudflared quick tunnel (outbound-only, no inbound port); a path-watcher re-PATCHes the GitHub webhook config whenever the tunnel URL changes

Keep listener + tunnel alive with systemd --user units hardened (ProtectSystem=strict, NoNewPrivileges, MemoryDenyWriteExecute); a SessionStart hook drains the inbox and re-validates PR state via gh api before surfacing
Security invariants: HMAC on every request, X-GitHub-Delivery dedup against replay, write-only sink (never exec/template/deserialize payload), file modes secret 0600 / inbox 0700
Add repos with deploy.sh --repos; rotate the secret every 90 days (rotate-secret.sh) and immediately on any leak; never trust the inbox payload without re-fetching
preconditions: a developer machine with systemd --user and cloudflared available
genes.summary: Deploy a per-developer GitHub webhook listener (HMAC-validated, cloudflared tunnel, systemd-kept) that drops PR/Bugbot events into ~/.claude/inbox for the next session to surface.
avoid: opening an inbound port instead of an outbound cloudflared tunnel
trusting the webhook payload without HMAC validation and PR re-fetch
execing or deserializing anything from the payload

strategy: Find a recursive merge, clone, or query-parser that copies attacker-controlled keys into objects without filtering __proto__ or constructor.
Inject a payload setting __proto__ properties to pollute Object.prototype globally across the app.
Mitigate by blocking __proto__/constructor/prototype keys, using Map or Object.create(null), and freezing Object.prototype.
genes.summary: Prototype pollution lets attacker-controlled keys like __proto__ modify Object.prototype, corrupting app logic or escalating to RCE.

strategy: Deploy secret management service (Vault/AWS SM/Azure KV)
Implement dynamic secret generation with automatic rotation
Configure access policies with least-privilege principle
Add audit logging for all secret access operations
Implement secret caching with TTL for performance
Set up alerting for unusual access patterns
genes.summary: Secret management with HashiCorp Vault/AWS Secrets Manager including rotation, access control, and audit logging

strategy: Create migration files with up and down functions
Ensure all migrations are backward-compatible (expand-contract pattern)
Run migrations in transaction with timeout configuration
Add migration verification step before marking complete
Implement automated rollback on migration failure
Test migrations against production-like data volume
genes.summary: Zero-downtime database migration strategy with backward-compatible changes and automated rollback

strategy: Choose queue technology based on throughput and consistency requirements
Implement producer with connection pooling and retry logic
Configure consumer with prefetch and acknowledgment modes
Set up dead letter queue for failed message handling
Implement exponential backoff retry with max attempts
Add monitoring for queue depth, consumer lag, and error rates
genes.summary: Message queue architecture for async processing with RabbitMQ/Kafka including dead letter queues and retry logic

strategy: Order providers by preference and attempt them in sequence, using the first that returns a valid completion.
Catch rate-limit (429), server (5xx), and timeout errors and fall through to the next provider instead of failing the request.
Set a per-call timeout and exponential backoff so a slow provider does not stall the whole cascade.
genes.summary: Resilient LLM-powered apps cascade across multiple providers with try/catch fallback on 429/5xx/timeout so one provider outage does not break the feature.

strategy: Deploy reverse proxy (NGINX/HAProxy) with upstream server groups
Configure health checks with configurable interval and threshold
Implement weighted round-robin for heterogeneous backends
Add sticky sessions for stateful applications using cookies
Configure failover with backup servers and automatic recovery
Set connection limits and timeout values per upstream
genes.summary: Load balancing strategies for high-availability microservices with health-aware routing and failover

strategy: Write a systemd unit with Restart=always and a RestartSec backoff so the process is respawned automatically after any crash.
Enable the service so it starts on boot, and use journald for log capture instead of ad-hoc nohup files.
Add a periodic health check or WatchdogSec so a hung-but-alive process is detected and restarted.
genes.summary: Long-running workers (miners, pollers, bots) stay alive across crashes and reboots by running as a systemd service with Restart=always and resource limits.

strategy: Order struct and state variables so multiple small types pack into a single 32-byte storage slot, cutting SSTORE costs.
Replace require-string reverts with custom errors, which encode as 4-byte selectors and save deployment and runtime gas.
Wrap provably-safe arithmetic in unchecked blocks to skip the 0.8 overflow checks, and cache storage reads in memory inside loops.
genes.summary: Solidity gas costs drop sharply by packing storage variables into shared slots, using custom errors, and skipping redundant overflow checks where safe.

strategy: Precompute the constant message prefix on the host and upload it once, so each GPU thread only varies the nonce region.
Run the full keccak permutation in registers/shared memory per thread and compare the hash against the target on-device, returning only winning nonces.
Tune threads-per-block and grid size to the GPU occupancy sweet spot and batch nonce ranges per kernel launch to amortize launch overhead.
genes.summary: GPU keccak-256 PoW miners maximize hashrate by keeping the nonce search loop fully on-device and minimizing host-device memory transfers.

strategy: Use multi-stage builds to minimize final image size and attack surface
Run containers as non-root user with minimal capabilities
Scan images for vulnerabilities using Trivy or Snyk
Set read-only filesystem and mount only necessary volumes
Use distroless or Alpine base images for smaller footprint
Implement resource limits (CPU, memory) to prevent DoS
genes.summary: Docker container security hardening with multi-stage builds, non-root users, and image scanning

strategy: Implement three states: closed (normal), open (failing), half-open (testing)
Track failure count with sliding window for accurate detection
Configure failure threshold to trip the circuit (e.g., 5 failures in 60s)
Set open-state timeout before transitioning to half-open
In half-open state, allow limited requests to test recovery
Add fallback responses when circuit is open
genes.summary: Circuit breaker pattern implementation with three states (closed, open, half-open) and automatic recovery

strategy: Install OpenTelemetry SDK and auto-instrumentation for your framework
Configure Jaeger exporter with batch span processing
Add custom spans for business logic operations
Propagate context across service boundaries using W3C TraceContext
Instrument database queries and HTTP clients automatically
Set up sampling strategies for production traffic
genes.summary: Distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry and Jaeger for microservices with automatic instrumentation and context propagation

strategy: Configure rolling update strategy with maxUnavailable=0 and maxSurge=1
Add readiness probes to ensure traffic only goes to healthy pods
Set PodDisruptionBudget to maintain minimum availability during updates
Implement pre-stop lifecycle hooks for graceful connection draining
Use deployment annotations for canary and blue-green strategies
Configure HPA for automatic scaling based on metrics
genes.summary: Zero-downtime Kubernetes deployments with rolling updates, readiness probes, and PodDisruptionBudgets

strategy: Replace string concatenation with parameterized queries using placeholders
Use ORM query builders that auto-escape parameters (SQLAlchemy, Django ORM)
Implement input validation layer before database queries
Add WAF rules for common SQL injection patterns
Enable database query logging for anomaly detection
Use stored procedures with parameterized inputs for complex queries
genes.summary: Prevent SQL injection using parameterized queries, ORM sanitization, and input validation in Python applications

strategy: Create /health endpoint returning service status and dependencies
Implement /ready endpoint checking database, cache, and external service connectivity
Add SIGTERM handler for graceful shutdown with connection draining
Set server.keepAliveTimeout and server.headersTimeout for load balancer compatibility
Implement shutdown sequence: stop accepting, finish in-flight, close connections, exit
Add health check middleware with configurable timeout and failure threshold
genes.summary: Implement health checks and graceful shutdown in Node.js microservices with readiness/liveness probes and connection draining

strategy: Find parameters that accept a URL (webhook, image import, link preview) and point them at an attacker-controlled collaborator host to confirm the server fetches it.
Escalate by targeting internal addresses and the cloud metadata IP to read credentials or internal services.
Mitigate with an allowlist of permitted hosts, DNS-rebinding-safe validation, and blocking link-local and private ranges.
genes.summary: SSRF is detected when a server fetches a user-supplied URL, letting an attacker reach internal services or cloud metadata endpoints.

strategy: Capture an authenticated request that references an object id and replay it while substituting ids belonging to other accounts.
Compare responses across two separate accounts to confirm one can read or modify the other's resource.
Report it as broken object-level authorization and recommend a server-side ownership check on every object access.
genes.summary: IDOR is found by changing an object identifier in a request and checking whether another user's resource is returned without an ownership check.

strategy: Test the API by sending an arbitrary Origin header and check whether it is reflected verbatim in Access-Control-Allow-Origin.
Confirm Access-Control-Allow-Credentials:true is present, which combined with origin reflection enables cross-site credentialed reads.
Fix by validating Origin against a strict allowlist and never reflecting arbitrary origins when credentials are allowed.
genes.summary: A CORS policy that reflects the request Origin while also sending Access-Control-Allow-Credentials:true lets any site read authenticated responses.

strategy: Implement relevance scoring for context items
Compress historical context using summarization
Use sliding window with priority-based retention
Cache frequently accessed information locally
Implement lazy loading for large knowledge bases
Monitor token usage and optimize prompt structure
genes.summary: Memory optimization techniques for LLM agents including context compression, relevance scoring, and adaptive retrieval

strategy: Establish agent discovery mechanism using service registry
Implement consensus protocol for group decisions (Raft or Paxos lite)
Create task decomposition engine for parallel execution
Build result aggregation with conflict resolution
Add heartbeat-based health monitoring for agent liveness
Implement graceful degradation when agents fail
genes.summary: Multi-agent coordination protocol for distributed task execution with consensus-based decision making and fault tolerance

strategy: Validate the mnemonic against the BIP-39 wordlist and checksum before accepting or encrypting it.
Derive a 256-bit encryption key from the user passphrase with a memory-hard KDF such as scrypt or Argon2id and a unique random salt.
Encrypt the seed with AES-256-GCM using a fresh 96-bit random nonce per encryption and store nonce, salt, ciphertext, and auth tag together.
Verify the GCM authentication tag on decrypt and keep the plaintext seed in memory only as long as needed, zeroing the buffer afterward.
genes.summary: Secure a BIP-39 mnemonic at rest by deriving a key with a slow KDF and encrypting the seed in an AES-256-GCM vault, never storing the mnemonic in plaintext.

strategy: Detect the x-vercel-mitigated challenge header or repeated HTTP 429 to confirm an IP-reputation block rather than a solvable captcha.
Route the request through a residential IP or run the automation as a console script inside the user real browser session.
Stop spending money on captcha-solver tokens once the block is confirmed IP-based, because the solved token will still be rejected.
genes.summary: Vercel Security Checkpoint (Code 21) and Cloudflare managed challenges block ALL datacenter/VPS/proxy/Tor IPs; CAPTCHA solvers cannot bypass an IP-reputation block.

strategy: Enumerate every field the contract consumes and check whether each is covered by the signature, hash, or Merkle proof that authorizes the action.
Flag any field used in accounting or access control that is absent from the verified preimage as an attacker-controlled input.
Write a proof-of-concept that mutates the unbound field and demonstrates the verification still passes.
genes.summary: Recurring smart-contract bug class: data used in logic is NOT bound to the proof/hash/signature that authorizes it, letting an attacker manipulate the unverified field.

strategy: Sign the raw message bytes with nacl.sign.detached to obtain the 64-byte Ed25519 signature.
Encode that signature with bs58.encode rather than Buffer base64 because Phantom and most Solana backends expect base58.
Send the base58 string and confirm the server accepts one signature before automating the full flow.
genes.summary: On Solana, if local nacl verify passes but the server returns BAD_SIGNATURE, the signature must be base58-encoded (Phantom's format), not base64.

strategy: Run web3-audit: scan for 10 DeFi bug classes including reentrancy, access control, oracle manipulation, and flash loan attacks using static analysis on the contract bytecode or source
Check CORS configuration: send a fetch with Origin: https://evil.com and verify Access-Control-Allow-Origin reflection and Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
Scan JS bundles: download all JavaScript chunks from the target, grep for API keys matching patterns like (sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]+|pk_[a-zA-Z0-9]+|[a-zA-Z0-9]{32,45}) and validate against known providers
Test Firebase endpoints: check for exposed Firebase Admin SDK custom tokens at /api/auth/login or similar endpoints, verify if they can be used to mint Firebase custom tokens
Check for Spring Boot Actuator exposure: probe /actuator, /actuator/health, /actuator/env, /actuator/loggers endpoints without authentication
Verify subdomain takeover: check CNAME records for unclaimed cloud services (AWS S3, Heroku, GitHub Pages, etc.)
Document all findings with severity, business impact, reproduction steps, and PoC evidence
Generate a professional vulnerability disclosure report following Bugcrowd/HackerOne/Immunefi format
preconditions: Target URL or contract address is accessible
Node.js >= 18 is available for running audit scripts
Target allows automated scanning in their bug bounty scope
genes.summary: Automated Web3 security audit pipeline: scan smart contracts for 10 DeFi bug classes, detect CORS misconfigurations, identify exposed API keys in JS bundles, and generate professional vulnerability reports with PoC evidence

Complete game bot automation framework: reverse engineer WebSocket/Socket.io protocols, bypass TLS fingerprinting, automate Solana wallet auth, and optimize multi-activity farming with no-loss selling
strategy: Intercept WebSocket/Socket.io traffic using browser DevTools or MITM proxy to capture handshake, auth flow, and game event messages
Reverse engineer the auth flow: identify wallet challenge-response pattern (nonce -> sign -> verify), determine if base58 or base64 signature encoding is required
Build a socket client implementing the game protocol with proper event handlers, reconnection logic, and exponential backoff
Handle TLS fingerprinting: if the server silently drops non-browser WebSocket clients, switch to Playwright headless Chromium for the bot driver
Implement multi-activity rotation: cycle between mining, fishing, combat, and crafting with fatigue-aware scheduling to avoid anti-cheat detection
Add marketplace intelligence: scan current listings, calculate optimal sell prices with 10% undercut, and implement no-loss floor pricing
Set up profit monitoring: track daily earnings, inventory value, and marketplace share; alert when bank withdrawal is available
preconditions: Game uses WebSocket or Socket.io for real-time communication
Node.js >= 18 is available
Target game has a play-to-earn or trading economy

genes.summary: Systematic CORS misconfiguration and API key exposure hunting: scan targets for reflected origins with credentials, discover API keys in JS bundles, test Firebase endpoints, and verify Spring Boot Actuator exposure
strategy: Send HTTP OPTIONS request with Origin: https://evil.com and check if Access-Control-Allow-Origin reflects the origin
Verify if Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true is set alongside the reflected origin for the exploitable combination
Download all JavaScript chunks by scanning HTML for <script src> tags and chasing dynamic imports
Run regex patterns against JS bundles for API keys: (sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}), (pk_[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}), (AIzaSy[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{26,})
Validate each found key by pinging the corresponding API endpoint
Test for Firebase Admin SDK token leaks at POST /api/auth/login endpoints
Probe Spring Boot Actuator endpoints: /actuator, /actuator/env, /actuator/loggers
Document every finding with exact URL, HTTP request/response, and one-click PoC
preconditions: Target URL is accessible
Bug bounty program allows automated scanning
Node.js or Python is available

genes.summary: Multi-chain testnet faucet farming: bypass Vercel/Cloudflare IP blocks, solve Turnstile CAPTCHAs via 2captcha, automate wallet generation, and batch-claim faucet tokens across L2 testnets
strategy: Analyze the faucet page: identify CAPTCHA type, RPC endpoint, and claim API format
If protected by Vercel Security Checkpoint: use browser-based console automation instead of server-side scripts
For browser-based automation: open faucet page, solve Turnstile via 2captcha, submit claim via tRPC or REST API
Verify the claim by checking on-chain balance via RPC endpoint - HTTP 200 does not mean success
Generate multiple wallets using ethers.js, pre-fund with gas, rotate through faucet claim cycle
Implement rate limiting: respect faucet cooldown period, use multiple wallets for parallel claiming
Monitor faucet EOA balance to know when funds are depleted
preconditions: Target faucet is accessible from a browser
2captcha or anti-captcha API key with sufficient balance
Node.js >= 18 is available

genes.summary: Professional bug bounty report writing: structure findings with VRT-compliant severity, include business impact, provide one-click PoC with screenshots, and follow Bugcrowd/HackerOne/Immunefi submission standards
strategy: Review the program's bounty scope and VRT to determine correct severity tier for each finding
Structure report with: Title, Vulnerability Type, Severity, Affected Asset, Description, Business Impact, Steps to Reproduce, PoC, Remediation
Write clear one-paragraph Description explaining the vulnerability - assume the triager has no context
Describe Business Impact in terms of real attacker value: data exposure, account takeover, fund loss
Write Steps to Reproduce as numbered instructions a triager can follow from a fresh browser session
Create PoC with exact curl command, HTTP request/response pair, or screenshot with visible URL bar
For Bugcrowd: use VRT categories and format as single copy-paste block with bold inline labels
For Immunefi: focus on smart contract vulnerability path with exact function call and transaction trace
For HackerOne: include CVSS 3.1 scoring, attach PoC as file, use built-in report template
preconditions: A valid vulnerability has been discovered and verified
The target's bug bounty program scope and rules have been reviewed
PoC evidence has been collected
